data.title: 10x Research: Crypto Market Reaches Key Turning Point; Altcoin Rotation and Low Selling Pressure Support Market Resilience
data.description: 10x Research, in an analysis published on the X platform, points out that the crypto market is at a critical inflection point. While Bitcoin maintains support levels, altcoins have begun to quietly dominate the market, and market misalignment may cause "buy the dip" strategies to frequently fail. Specifically, Ethereum may be supported by institutional buying and structural upgrades; Solana by ETF inflows; XRP by retail demand and application expansion; BNB under short-term pressure but benefiting from RWA's long-term development; and TRON by the growth of payment applications, stablecoin usage, and whale buying. At this stage, returns will depend more on tactical position management than directional judgment. As Bitcoin's dominance declines, the strategy has shifted to prioritizing altcoins that have stabilized at key levels, capitalizing on high-risk continuations or failed trends. In a bear market environment, the focus should be on short-term momentum trading rather than blindly buying the dip. Meanwhile, reduced token unlocking pressure and low trading volume indicate limited selling pressure, supporting overall market resilience.

data.title: Whale Opens 10x Leveraged Short on Hyperliquid
data.description: A significant investor has initiated a 10x leveraged short position on Hyperliquid, following a deposit of 3 million USDC. According to NS3.AI, the position is valued at approximately $9 million and currently holds 226,130 HYPE.

data.title: U.S. House Financial Services Committee to Hold Tokenization Hearing Next Week
data.description: The U.S. House Financial Services Committee is set to conduct a hearing on tokenization next Wednesday at 10:00 AM Eastern Time. According to NS3.AI, Summer Mersinger, CEO of the Blockchain Association, will participate as an invited witness. The hearing aims to explore the implications and potential of tokenization in financial markets.

data.title: Slovak Prime Minister Warns of Potential Oil Crisis Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: Following recent military actions by the United States and Israel against Iran, shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz continue to affect global energy supplies. According to Jin10, Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico issued a warning on March 20, highlighting the risk of an oil crisis in Europe due to multiple compounding factors. Since the strikes on Iran, the European Union has incurred additional costs exceeding 6 billion euros due to rising fuel prices. Fico emphasized that these developments, combined with Europe's current economic situation and substantial support for Ukraine, create a 'volatile combination.' He cautioned that the future could be dire, with the potential for an oil crisis. Fico urged the EU to expedite the resumption of operations for the 'Friendship' oil pipeline.

data.title: A whale deposited 3 million USDC into Hyperliquid to open a 10x leveraged short position in HYPE.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale deposited 3 million USDC into Hyperliquid to open a 10x leveraged short position in HYPE. The current position holds 226,310 HYPE, worth approximately $9 million. This whale had previously shorted HYPE, holding the position for as long as 48 days, but ultimately closed out the position with a loss of nearly $200,000.

data.title: Expert Advocates Risk-Based Approach for Broker Regulations
data.description: Chen Zhihua has highlighted the ongoing debate regarding brokers' requirement for clients to pre-register designated bank accounts, suggesting it may indicate the application of virtual asset regulatory concepts to traditional securities. According to NS3.AI, Chen emphasized that regulators should adopt a risk-based anti-money laundering strategy, concentrating on beneficial owners, abnormal fund flows, same-name account standards, and AI monitoring.

data.title: Crypto journalist: The U.S. Congressional Financial Services Committee is scheduled to hold a hearing on tokenization next Wednesday.
data.description: Crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett wrote on the X platform that the U.S. Congressional Financial Services Committee plans to hold a hearing on tokenization next Wednesday at 10 a.m. (Eastern Time), and Blockchain Association CEO Summer Mersinger will attend as an invited witness.

data.title: Cuba Prepares for Potential U.S. Aggression, Says President
data.description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel announced on March 20 that Cuba is preparing for potential aggression from the United States. According to Jin10, during a meeting with foreign activists delivering humanitarian aid to Cuba, Díaz-Canel acknowledged the possibility of aggression against the country. Earlier that day, Cuban Deputy Foreign Minister Carlos Fernández de Cossío stated at a press conference that Cuba's political system is not open for negotiation with the United States, nor are the positions of the Cuban President or any Cuban officials. This comes a week after Cuba indicated it had begun dialogue with the U.S. government.

data.title: Indonesia's President Prabowo Subianto Addresses Misunderstandings in Financial Sector
data.description: Indonesia's President Prabowo Subianto addressed criticisms in a recent interview, stating that he has been misunderstood by significant figures in the financial industry. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Subianto's remarks about the challenges he faces in conveying his policies and intentions to global financial leaders. He emphasized the importance of clear communication and understanding between Indonesia and international financial entities to foster better economic relations. Subianto remains committed to improving these relationships and ensuring that his administration's economic strategies are accurately perceived by the global market.

data.title: BONKfun is back online: Users will receive 110% compensation for losses suffered due to a social engineering attack.
data.description: The BONKfun team announced on the X platform that its official website was attacked by malicious social engineering on March 11. The attackers hijacked the BONKfun domain through the domain service provider and transferred it to an external registrar. The team confirmed that this incident was not caused by a breach of BONK's or BONKfun's internal systems, codebase, or team accounts. Following the incident, the team immediately took measures: shutting down the website, coordinating with wallet service providers to mark the domain as malicious, and controlling the impact on users. This attack resulted in approximately $30,000 in losses for customers, and the team will compensate affected users at 110% to cover potential opportunity costs. The BONKfun domain and registration were fully restored by approximately 5 PM Eastern Time on March 18, and the main wallet provider's functions were restored on the evening of March 19. The website is now securely online. Currently, some antivirus software still marks the main domain as risky; the team is actively addressing this. For users unable to access the official website due to antivirus software issues, an alternative domain has also been launched and is functionally identical to the main site.

data.title: Iran Ready to Permit Japanese Vessels Through Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi announced that Iran is prepared to allow Japanese-related vessels to navigate the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision follows consultations between officials from both nations. The move is seen as a significant development in the diplomatic relations between Iran and Japan, potentially easing tensions in the strategically important waterway. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical passage for global oil shipments, and the assurance of safe passage for Japanese vessels could have implications for international trade and energy markets.

data.title: Analysis: Gold suffered its biggest weekly drop in 43 years; the rebound was short-lived.
data.description: According to Gate data, the gold rebound was short-lived, with a V-shaped intraday decline, falling more than 5% from its daily high and breaking below the important support level of $4,500. This week saw a plunge of over 10%, reaching a seven-week low and marking the largest weekly drop since March 1983. As of Friday's (March 20) New York close, spot gold fell 3.42% to $4,491.67 per ounce, a cumulative decline of over 10% for the week; COMEX gold futures fell 2.47% to $4,492 per ounce, a cumulative decline of over 11% for the week. Spot silver fell 6.8% to $67.897 per ounce, a cumulative decline of over 15% for the week; COMEX silver futures fell 4.78% to $67.810 per ounce, a cumulative decline of over 16% for the week.

data.title: The World Coin team is suspected of engaging in OTC trading, exchanging 35 million USDC for 117 million WLD tokens.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, the World Coin team appears to have been conducting OTC transactions. They received 35 million USDC from FalconX and Binance, and then transferred 117 million WLD tokens to an OTC trading address. These tokens may also have been used by market makers.

data.title: Sam Altman Wins Dismissal of Sexual Abuse Lawsuit
data.description: OpenAI's CEO, Sam Altman, has successfully had a lawsuit dismissed that was filed by his sister, alleging sexual abuse over nearly ten years. Bloomberg posted on X that the court ruled in favor of Altman, leading to the dismissal of the case. The lawsuit had accused Altman of long-term abuse, but the court found insufficient grounds to proceed with the claims. This legal victory marks a significant development for Altman, who has been a prominent figure in the tech industry.

data.title: President of the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Professionals Association: The regulatory approach for virtual assets should not be applied to the traditional securities industry.
data.description: Chan Chi-wah, president of the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Professionals Association, commented on the controversy surrounding the "pre-registration of designated bank accounts" for brokerage clients. He stated that while the regulatory circular proposed establishing a bank account registration mechanism with caps, this practice may stem from the inappropriate application of virtual asset regulatory approaches (such as pre-approval of wallet addresses) to the traditional securities industry. Chan pointed out that while pre-approval is technically justifiable for virtual assets due to the difficulty in instantly verifying ownership of blockchain addresses, traditional securities fund flows can already be confirmed through mechanisms such as "same-name account verification," eliminating the need for a blanket restriction on the number of accounts. In contrast to the EU's anti-money laundering framework, the regulatory focus should be on penetrating beneficial ownership and identifying abnormal transactions, rather than pre-restricting accounts. He also suggested that regulators adhere to a "risk-based" principle, focusing on abnormal fund flows (such as "layered transactions"), clarifying compliance standards for same-name accounts, and promoting the application of big data and AI in anti-money laundering monitoring. (Orange News)

data.title: New Zealand's Credit Rating Outlook Downgraded by Fitch Ratings
data.description: New Zealand's credit rating outlook has been downgraded to negative by Fitch Ratings due to concerns over government debt. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the agency's apprehensions about the country's fiscal trajectory. The decision reflects Fitch's assessment of New Zealand's increasing debt levels and the potential impact on its economic stability. This move comes amid broader global economic uncertainties and challenges faced by various nations in managing public finances. Fitch's revision underscores the need for New Zealand to address its fiscal policies to maintain economic resilience.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards: Coordinated Strikes on U.S. Bases
data.description:
On March 21, local time, Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) announced the launch of the 70th wave of the 'Real Commitment 4' operation. According to Jin10, the IRGC stated that they conducted coordinated attacks on five U.S. military bases using Qiam and Emad missiles, along with attack drones. The targeted bases included Prince Sultan Air Base, Al Dhafra Air Base, Ali Al Salem Air Base, Erbil Air Base, and the U.S. Navy's Fifth Fleet base.

The IRGC's statement also mentioned the use of Khaibar Shekan and Qadr missiles to strike key targets, including areas in Haifa and Tel Aviv, Israel. The statement reiterated Iran's commitment to an offensive strategy and emphasized its readiness to intensify actions against any sources of aggression threatening Iran's territorial integrity and national sovereignty. The IRGC affirmed that Iran is fully prepared to confront the current phase of conflict.

data.title: Evernode CEO: XRP's Price Stagnation Linked to Retail Dominance
data.description: Evernode CEO Ashish Birla has highlighted that XRP's price is not experiencing significant gains despite increased on-chain activity. According to NS3.AI, Birla attributes this stagnation to the predominance of retail traffic in XRP's usage. He suggests that a sustainable price increase would necessitate the involvement of banks, corporations, and institutional investors utilizing XRP for working capital and as a liquidity bridge.

data.title: The United States has conditionally eased sanctions on Iranian oil for 30 days.
data.description: The United States approved a 30-day authorization on March 20, local time, conditionally easing sanctions on Iranian oil products, allowing the delivery and sale of Iranian crude oil and petroleum products already shipped as of March 20. U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessenter stated that the Treasury Department is issuing a "narrow, short-term authorization" to allow the sale of Iranian oil currently stranded at sea. By temporarily releasing existing oil supplies, the U.S. will quickly provide approximately 140 million barrels of oil to the global market. The temporary, short-term authorization is strictly limited to oil already en route. (CCTV News)

data.title: Iran Launches Missiles at Diego Garcia Base, U.S. Military Responds
data.description: Iran has reportedly launched two medium-range ballistic missiles targeting the joint U.S.-U.K. military base at Diego Garcia in the central Indian Ocean, according to The Wall Street Journal. Both missiles failed to hit the base; one experienced a mid-flight failure, while the U.S. Navy intercepted the other with a defensive missile. This incident highlights ongoing tensions in the region and the strategic importance of the Diego Garcia base for U.S. and U.K. military operations.
